[00:00] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Hi, I am Dr Patel. You are here with Milo today?
[00:05] **Speaker 2 (Owner):** Yes, Milo, four year old Labrador, male neutered. He has had diarrhoea since yesterday morning.
[00:14] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** How many episodes?
[00:16] **Speaker 2 (Owner):** About seven yesterday and three this morning.
[00:20] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Stool character? Mucus or blood?
[00:23] **Speaker 2 (Owner):** Watery with mucus. Tiny red streak once last night.
[00:28] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Vomiting?
[00:29] **Speaker 2 (Owner):** One vomit yesterday afternoon, yellow foam, none since.
[00:34] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Appetite, thirst, energy?
[00:36] **Speaker 2 (Owner):** Reduced appetite, still drinking, a bit quieter but still interactive.
[00:43] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Any scavenging or dietary indiscretion?
[00:46] **Speaker 2 (Owner):** He grabbed sausage and bread at the park.
[00:50] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Any chocolate, grapes, onions, xylitol?
[00:53] **Speaker 2 (Owner):** No.
[00:55] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Current meds?
[00:57] **Speaker 2 (Owner):** I gave Pro-Kolin at home, about 2 ml twice yesterday.
[01:03] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Vaccinations up to date?
[01:05] **Speaker 2 (Owner):** Yes.
[01:07] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** I will examine him now.
[01:11] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Bright, responsive, friendly. Weight 28.4 kg. Temp 38.7 C, HR 108, RR 28.
[01:20] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** MM pink and moist, CRT under 2 seconds.
[01:24] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Mild cranial abdominal discomfort on deep palpation, no mass, no severe guarding.
[01:31] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Heart rhythm normal, lungs clear, pulses good.
[01:35] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Mild dehydration around five percent.
[01:39] **Speaker 2 (Owner):** Is that dangerous?
[01:41] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Stable for outpatient care today.
[01:45] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Impression is acute gastroenteritis, likely dietary indiscretion.
[01:50] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Plan is maropitant injection now, then Pro-Kolin 5 ml by mouth every 8 hours for 3 days.
[01:58] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Bland diet for 3 days, then transition over 2 to 3 days.
[02:03] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Encourage water. Avoid high fat treats and new foods.
[02:08] **Speaker 2 (Owner):** Midday dose is hard with work.
[02:10] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Try for q8h, partner can help if possible.
[02:14] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Red flags: repeated vomiting, worsening blood in stool, marked lethargy, worsening abdominal pain, or no improvement in 48 hours.
[02:23] **Speaker 2 (Owner):** Understood.
[02:25] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** We will book a nurse call tomorrow afternoon.
[02:29] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** If not improving, recheck and faecal testing.
[02:33] **Speaker 2 (Owner):** That works.
[02:35] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Reception will send written instructions by email and provide a stool pot.
[02:40] **Speaker 2 (Owner):** Perfect.
[02:42] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Nurse Sarah, please administer maropitant 1 mg/kg SC and dispense Pro-Kolin with q8h instructions.
[02:49] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** Also schedule nurse progress call tomorrow at 15:30.
[02:54] **Speaker 2 (Owner):** Thanks.
[03:00] **Speaker 1 (Vet):** End consult.
